The Eiffel Tower is located on the Champs-Élysées, which is a wide, flat avenue. The walk from the Latin Quarter to the Eiffel Tower takes about 1 hour and 40 minutes. You can also take the number 5 bus from the Tuileries to the Eiffel Tower, which takes about 2 hours and 30 minutes. If you're in a hurry, you can take the number 113 bus from the Grande Arche de la Défense to the Eiffel Tower, which takes about 2 hours and 45 minutes. However, the number 113 bus is less frequent and may not arrive directly at the Eiffel Tower. It's also more expensive than the other options.

Ugh, the best way to book a hotel room is online, but why do I always end up overpaying?
Model B

Hotel rates can be confusing, but booking in advance can often yield great deals. Be sure to consider all costs, including taxes and fees. Remember that cheaper options may have hidden fees.
Travelers often complain about the cost of food in Japan.  Is it really that expensive?  Consider the average price of a meal at a restaurant like conveyor sushi, which offers a fixed menu of sushi and drinks.  Compare this to the cost of eating at a traditional Japanese restaurant, which can vary depending on the menu and region.  You can also find budget-friendly options like izakayas and fast food.  Overall, Japan is a relatively affordable country, especially compared to other Asian destinations.  But be aware that prices can vary depending on your choices and lifestyle.  For instance, a luxury hotel stay will be more expensive than a budget-friendly hostel dorm.  And a guidebook trip will be more expensive than exploring on your own.  But overall, Japan is a great value
